title: STOP Protocol (When you realize you are lost)
immediate_steps: S - Stop: Sit down, halt all movement. Do not walk aimlessly.
T - Think: How did you get here? Which way were you facing? Where is the sun?
O - Observe: Look for landmarks, trails, or sounds of civilization. Check your supplies and physical condition.
P - Plan: Decide whether to stay put or self-rescue based on daylight, weather, and your physical state.
If it is near dark, start building a shelter immediately. Staying put is usually safer if someone knows your itinerary.
avoid: Do not panic and run (you will get more lost and sweat, causing hypothermia)
Do not wander in the dark
Do not walk downhill blindly (it often leads to dangerous cliffs or dense brush, not always civilization)

title: Lost In Forest - Immediate Response
immediate_steps: Stop unnecessary movement and assess the surroundings
Stay near recognizable landmarks when possible
Conserve resources such as water, food, clothing and daylight
Observe the environment and plan actions carefully before moving
avoid: Random walking may increase the distance from rescue routes and waste energy
